name: Relational Tables Access
body: [{"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "You can configure external MySQL client access to work with your Marketing Cloud's relational tables for advanced contact data management. This enables more relevant messaging through sophisticated data operations and helps organize complex audience information while maintaining full control over your subscriber data architecture."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "When properly configured, this setup streamlines your workflow for large-scale data operations while ensuring you understand the important limitations that affect segmentation capabilities and automated journey triggers within your marketing campaigns."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "You can use a MySQL client for creating and managing Maropost\u2019s relational tables. To use a MySQL client for accessing Maropost\u2019s relational tables, you are required to do a quick initial setup."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "The details required for the setup can be found on the Relational Tables page on the Settings page."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "To learn about relational tables, see "}, {"text": "Introduction to Relational Tables", "bold": true}, {"text": " and "}, {"text": "Creating Relational Tables", "bold": true}, {"text": "."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": ""}]}, {"type": "h2", "children": [{"text": "Setting Up Relational Table Access"}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Follow the steps to set up relational table access:"}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "1. Click your user name in the top-right corner of the application, and then from the drop-down menu, select "}, {"text": "Settings", "bold": true}, {"text": "."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "2. On the Settings page, in the Connections section, click "}, {"text": "Relational Tables", "bold": true}, {"text": "."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "3. On the Relational Tables page, you can find the details needed to set up the access."}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"type": "rich_embed_card", "children": [{"text": ""}], "dataSourceType": "image", "url": "https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/05T5g00000BkWsp.05T5g00000BkWspEAF.png", "embedData": {"url": "https://us.v-cdn.net/6038474/uploads/attachments/migrated/05T5g00000BkWsp.05T5g00000BkWspEAF.png", "name": "Relational_Tables_-_Settings.png", "embedType": "image", "float": "none", "height": 1268, "width": 2560}}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": ""}]}, {"type": "h2", "children": [{"text": "Restrictions When Using a MySQL Client"}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "Remember the following restrictions when using a MySQL client:"}]}, {"type": "ul", "children": [{"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "p", "children": [{"text": "The relational tables using MySQL Client are not\u00a0visible within the application. Therefore, you must create the tables and their relational links in the application if you intend to use the tables for segmentation or personalization."}]}]}, {"type": "li", "children": [{"type": "lic", "children": [{"text": "When you add, update, or delete data in the relational tables using MySQL Client, the 'Table Field Updated' Journey trigger doesn't\u00a0detect this event. Therefore, if you intend to use this trigger on a specific table, then you must use the application interface to add, update, and delete the data in that table."}]}]}]}, {"type": "p", "children": [{"text": ""}]}]
